kill_fact: No kill. Every load-bearing figure independently verifies against the Q1 2026 release (reported 2026-04-29, the most recent quarter as of 2026-06-22), so the STALE-ANCHOR vector fails: AWS +28% to $37.6B at a $14.2B / 37.7% segment op income; consolidated op income $23.9B / 13.1% margin; diluted EPS $2.78 explicitly flattered by a $16.8B pre-tax Anthropic non-operating gain; TTM FCF collapsed $25.9B -> $1.2B on a $59.3B YoY rise in net PP&E purchases; $364B AWS backlog ex-Anthropic; ads $17.2B/+24%. The bear's substantive concerns (near-zero FCF by choice, EPS inflated by the non-cash mark, AWS the #3 hyperscaler grower vs Azure +40% / GCP +63%, unproven ~$344B-buildout ROIC, Anthropic circularity) all verify -- but they are NOT hidden; they are already fully articulated in the dossier and priced into verdict B / WATCHLIST-for-capitulation. The sop_fair_value $245 sits only ~5% above the ~$232 tape -- not a fabricated discount, so there is nothing to refute.
corrections: (1) Q1 capex: bull cites $43.2B cash capex; the release headline figure is $44.2B (gross purchases of PP&E) -- immaterial, both confirm the ~$200B/2026 pace. (2) Prior-year EPS: bull implies a ~$1.63 consensus base for the YoY frame; actual Q1'25 diluted EPS was $1.59 -- immaterial. (3) value_conviction trimmed 4 -> 3: the FCF trough is real cash consumed now with depreciation drag ahead, AWS is demonstrably ceding relative cloud share, the AI-capex ROIC is unproven with no historical precedent, and this is a fair-value (not cheap) compounder into a single-AI-capex-factor-dominated tape -- the bull's 4 is one notch rich, consistent with the engine's own verdict B / conviction 3.
evidence: Amazon Q1 2026 Earnings Release (2026-04-29): net sales $181.5B (+17%); total operating income $23.9B (vs $18.4B Q1'25); AWS segment sales $37.6B (+28%), AWS op income $14.2B (vs $11.5B), 37.7% margin; net income $30.3B, diluted EPS $2.78 (vs $1.59 Q1'25) including a $16.8B pre-tax non-operating gain on the Anthropic investment.

Amazon Q1 2026 cash-flow disclosure (2026-04-29): trailing-twelve-month free cash flow $1.2B, down from $25.9B, driven by a $59.3B YoY increase in purchases of property and equipment net of proceeds; Q1 capex $44.2B (vs ~$25B Q1'25).
Amazon Q1 2026 management commentary (2026-04-29): AWS remaining-backlog ~$364B (excludes the Anthropic >$100B/10-yr commitment); advertising revenue $17.2B, +24% YoY.

Hyperscaler Q1 2026 cloud-growth cross-check (Apr 2026 reports): AWS +28%, Microsoft Azure +40%, Google Cloud +63% -- AWS confirmed the slowest-growing of the three, corroborating the relative-share-loss bear point.
